mission.title: Install and Configure ExternalDNS
mission.description: ExternalDNS is an operator that dynamically manages DNS records for Kubernetes resources like Services and Ingresses across various DNS providers. It simplifies the process of keeping DNS records in sync with Kubernetes resources, making it ideal for environments where services frequently change. Key trade-offs include reliance on external DNS providers and potential latency in DNS record updates.

steps.title: Add the ExternalDNS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ExternalDNS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add external-dns https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for ExternalDNS.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install ExternalDNS with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs ExternalDNS in the 'kube-system' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

```

> Change provider and zoneType as needed.
commands: helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates a sample ExternalDNS resource to manage DNS records for your services.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: example-external-dns
spec:
  provider: aws
  zoneType: public
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  service:
    annotations:
      external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/hostname: myservice.example.com
EOF

```

> Replace 'example.com' and 'myservice' with your actual domain and service name.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: example-external-dns
spec:
  provider: aws
  zoneType: public
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  service:
    annotations:
      external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/hostname: myservice.example.com
EOF

steps.title: Verify ExternalDNS is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ExternalDNS deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> You should see the ExternalDNS pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ExternalDNS
uninstall.description:
This step will remove ExternalDNS and may lead to orphaned DNS records in your DNS provider. Ensure to clean up any DNS records manually if needed.

```bash

helm uninstall external-dns --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f example-external-dns.yaml

```

> This will remove the ExternalDNS release.
> Ensure to delete the Custom Resource if created.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ExternalDNS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your current configuration and verify the current version. This step will upgrade ExternalDNS to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get deployment external-dns -n kube-system -o yaml > external-dns-backup.yaml

helm upgrade external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0

kubectl rollout status deployment/external-dns -n kube-system

```

> Backup the current deployment configuration.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgrade was successful.
commands: kubectl get deployment external-dns -n kube-system -o yaml > external-dns-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create record set'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the IAM role does not have sufficient permissions to modify DNS records.

**Fix:**
Ensure the IAM role associated with the ExternalDNS pod has the necessary permissions for Route 53.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No matching records found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that ExternalDNS is not able to find any Services or Ingresses to manage.

**Fix:**
Check if your Services or Ingresses have the correct annotations for ExternalDNS.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen due to misconfiguration in the ExternalDNS deployment.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the ExternalDNS pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: DNS records not updating
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to rate limiting by the DNS provider.

**Fix:**
Review the DNS provider's rate limits and adjust the ExternalDNS configuration accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Azure DNS and improved AWS Route 53 integration.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the ExternalDNS pod running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
